frameworks.name: lateral intracortical connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Long-distance horizontal V1 connections shape subthreshold surround responses.

frameworks.name: psychophysical association field
frameworks.one_line: Cortical analog of collinear contour-grouping field mapped in V1 synaptic input.

frameworks.name: top-down binding hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: Higher-area top-down binding contrasted with bottom-up V1 lateral integration.

frameworks.name: dynamic association field
frameworks.one_line: Proposed motion-dependent extension of static association field at saccadic speeds.

core_question: Do long-distance horizontal connections in V1 mediate low-level perceptual binding of form and motion across the visual field?
key_assumptions: subthreshold synaptic responses reveal lateral functional connectivity beyond spiking RF
anesthetized cat V1 is a valid model for low-level perceptual integration
in-phase summation of horizontal and feedforward inputs indexes facilitation
manipulations: oriented stimulus position across nonspiking RF surround
motion direction (centripetal vs centrifugal) along preferred-orientation axis
key_findings: subthreshold surround responses to oriented flashes cluster geometrically around the preferred-orientation axis
this functional bias mirrors the psychophysical association field for collinear contours
apparent-motion stimuli evoke facilitatory nonlinear interactions when horizontal and feedforward inputs sum in-phase
facilitation is specific to centripetal collinear activation at saccadic-like speeds
interpretations: V1 lateral connectivity implements a dynamic association field binding form and motion at low level
low-level perceptual grouping does not require top-down higher-area binding
